one_liner: INTC -5.25% to $120 — intraday low of $117.63 briefly pierced the April SC floor ($118.50); the turnaround thesis holds but only if that level is defended on a closing basis.

news_catalyst.headline: Broadcom guidance miss + weak jobs report; INTC now -15% from $142 record three sessions ago
news_catalyst.summary: Intel is being hit by the same dual macro catalysts as Micron — Broadcom's Q3 AI guidance miss and the weak 172K jobs print. INTC's vulnerability is its transitional status: burning cash on 18A, not yet a clear AI chip winner, and acutely sensitive to enterprise AI spending sentiment. The stock hit a record $142 on June 30 on turnaround optimism; three sessions later it is at $120, -15% from the high. The intraday low of $117.63 briefly violated the April 24 SC floor at $118.50 — the most critical Wyckoff event in the dataset.

technical.summary: The April 24 SC (2.55x, 281M shares — the dataset maximum) established $118.50 as the accumulation floor. Today's $117.63 low pierced it intraday before recovering to $120.35 close. This intraday violation that recovered is a critical data point: either (a) it's a Spring (shakeout below SC to flush final sellers before markup) or (b) the SC floor has cracked and distribution continues. Volume near-average (0.97x) on a -5.25% day slightly favors the Spring interpretation — a true breakdown would see more urgency from sellers.

wyckoff_narrative: Today's intraday test of $117.63 is either a Wyckoff Secondary Test (ST) of the April SC low — which is constructive if it holds on a closing basis — or the beginning of a Phase C breakdown. The distinguishing factor will be volume on the next few sessions: drying volume with higher lows = Spring setup. Expanding volume with lower lows = distribution breakdown. The $118.50 level on a closing basis is the line.

valuation.summary: DCF not feasible — Intel is burning cash during the 18A foundry transition. Cantor Fitzgerald maintains a $150 price target based on the turnaround narrative. No fundamental floor from DCF.

risks: Intraday low $117.63 pierced the April SC floor — must not close below $117-118 on a subsequent session
Sentiment score -0.467 (most bearish of non-ASTS names in watchlist)
